title: Of Abraham.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: From this rises the appellation of the Hebrews.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: For the word Hebrews is interpreted to mean those who migrate across, viz., who crossed the Euphrates with Abraham; and it is not derived, as some think, from the fore-mentioned Heber.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: From the flood and Noe, therefore, to Abraham’s entrance into the promised land, there are in all 1015 years; and from Adam, in 20 generations 3277 years.
